logo_link_label: Dataset Register – Dutch Heritage Network (go to homepage)
distribution_rdf: This dataset provides an RDF download
distribution_sparql: This dataset provides a SPARQL endpoint
sparql_view_query: View SPARQL query in the triple store
publisher_explanation: The organization that publishes or created the dataset.
format_explanation: Formats in which the dataset is made available for downloading or querying.
class_explanation: Resource classes contained in the dataset. Only available if the dataset is distributed as RDF or SPARQL.
terminology_source_explanation: Terminology sources that the dataset links to. Only available if the dataset is distributed as RDF or SPARQL.
size_explanation: Number of triples in the dataset. Only available if the dataset is distributed as RDF or SPARQL.
rss_subscribe: Subscribe to RSS feed for this search query
detail_registered_description: Date at which the URL was registered with the Dataset Register.
detail_last_crawled_description: When the URL was last read by the Dataset Register.
detail_quality_rating_description: Indicates how complete the dataset description is based on the metadata properties provided, such as title, description, license, and publisher.
detail_rating_explanation: Improve your dataset's visibility by adding more metadata properties.
detail_archived_message: This dataset is no longer actively maintained and may be out of date.
detail_uri_description: The unique identifier (URI) for this dataset.
detail_publisher_description: Organization or individual that provides the dataset.
detail_spatial_coverage_description: Indicates the place(s) which are the focus of the dataset.
detail_temporal_coverage_description: Indicates the period that the dataset applies to.
detail_classes_description: The distribution of RDF types (classes) in the dataset. Click a class to see its properties.
detail_no_properties: No property information available for this class.
detail_properties_section_description: Properties (predicates) used in this dataset. Click to see which classes use each property.
detail_vocabularies_description: Vocabularies that predicates in the dataset refer to.
detail_terminology_sources_description: Terminology sources used by objects in the dataset.
detail_object_types_description: Shows the distribution between literal values and URI references in the dataset's object positions.
detail_linked_data_summary_description: Statistical information about this dataset retrieved from the Knowledge Graph, including the number of triples, entities, properties, and class distribution.
detail_registered_url_description: URL where the dataset is described.
detail_verified_tooltip: This distribution has been validated by the Dataset Knowledge Graph.
detail_distributions_tooltip: Distributions provide access to the data.
detail_is_part_of_description: The data catalog this dataset belongs to.
facets_status_invalid_tooltip: Datasets whose description no longer conforms to the requirements.
facets_status_gone_tooltip: Datasets whose registration URL is no longer accessible.
facets_status_explanation: By default only current datasets are shown. Here you can choose to view datasets that are no longer valid or no longer exist.
detail_value_types_description: The distribution of data types for literal values and classes for URI objects in this dataset.
detail_language_description: Language or languages in which the dataset is available.
dataset_schema_ap_nde: Conforms to the NDE Schema.org Application Profile (sampled)
nde_compatibility_description: These checks run automatically against the current NDE criteria. They give an up-to-date picture and help you improve the findability and reusability of your dataset.
nde_compat_registration_explanation_regi: This dataset’s description is registered in the Dataset Register, so it can be found and reused.
nde_compat_registration_explanation_gone: The registration URL is no longer accessible, so the description can no longer be retrieved.
nde_compat_registration_explanation_inva: The description no longer conforms to the requirements for datasets.
nde_compat_registration_explanation_warn: This dataset’s description is registered and valid, but it validated with warnings: some recommendations for datasets are not yet met.
nde_compat_persistent_explanation_persis: This dataset’s URIs resolve to a landing page.
nde_compat_persistent_explanation_resolv: This dataset’s URIs use a non-persistent domain name, for example one from a software vendor.
nde_compat_persistent_explanation_unreso: Some of this dataset’s sampled URIs do not resolve to a landing page.
nde_compat_persistent_explanation_pendin: This dataset’s URIs have not been checked for persistence yet.

match.countPlural=one: {unresolved} of {display} sampled URIs does not resolve.
match.countPlural=other: {unresolved} of {display} sampled URIs do not resolve.

nde_compat_linked_data_heading_conforms: Provides linked data conforming to the NDE Application Profile
nde_compat_linked_data_explanation_confo: This dataset provides linked data conforming to the NDE Schema.org Application Profile, making the data easy to find and reuse.
nde_compat_linked_data_explanation_not_c: This dataset provides linked data, but it does not yet conform to the NDE Schema.org Application Profile.
nde_compat_linked_data_explanation_pendi: This dataset provides linked data. Its content has not been assessed yet.
nde_compat_linked_data_explanation_none: This dataset provides no linked data, which makes the data harder to reuse.
nde_compat_linked_data_explanation_empty: This dataset declares linked data, but its content could not be retrieved.
nde_compat_iiif_explanation: With IIIF, the images in a dataset can be viewed in any IIIF viewer.

match.countPlural=one: {display} manifest declared, but it could not be validated.
match.countPlural=other: {display} manifests declared, but none of the sampled manifests could be validated.

nde_compat_terms_heading_uses: Uses terms from the Network of Terms
nde_compat_terms_heading_not_uses: Does not use terms from the Network of Terms
nde_compat_terms_explanation_before: Objects in this dataset link to shared terms from terminology sources in the
nde_compat_terms_explanation_after: , which makes the data interoperable and easier to find.
validate_page_intro_before: Have you created and published your dataset description? And do you want to know if it meets the
validate_page_intro_after: ? And do you have a NDE-compatible collection information system? Then this validation will happen automatically. Why is this important? Only descriptions that meet the requirements will be included in the Dataset Register.
validate_page_intro_non_nde: If you don't have a NDE-compatible collection information system, you can validate your dataset description below. Enter the URL of the page where you have published your dataset description. After validation, you will see immediately if your dataset description meets the requirements of the Dataset Register. You will also receive suggestions for improvement. If your dataset description is not yet published, copy and paste the machine-readable dataset description into the Paste tab. Select the correct content type and let the description be validated.
validate_format_unsupported: Automatic formatting isn’t available for this content type.
validate_result_not_found_body: The Dataset Register couldn’t resolve this URL. Check that it is publicly reachable and returns a dataset description.
validate_result_no_dataset_body: The URL resolves, but no schema:Dataset or dcat:Dataset was found. See the requirements for details.
validate_result_fetch_failed_body: The Dataset Register couldn’t fetch this URL. Check that it is publicly reachable and serves RDF (for example via content negotiation for application/ld+json or text/turtle).
validate_summary_valid_body: Your dataset description meets the requirements.
validate_summary_invalid_body: Your dataset description has issues that need to be fixed before it can be registered.
validate_summary_warnings_only_body: No violations, so the description can be registered. The recommendations below help consumers discover, understand and reuse your dataset and keep your description robust when the requirements evolve.
